baya-weaver.habitat: Grasslands, cultivated areas, scrub, and secondary growth across the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia
baya-weaver.diet: Seeds and grain; forages in flocks
baya-weaver.migration: Largely resident; prone to local, seasonal movements in response to rain and food availability

scaly-breasted-munia.habitat: Tropical plains and grasslands from India and Sri Lanka east to Indonesia and the Philippines
scaly-breasted-munia.diet: Mainly grass seeds, supplemented by berries and small insects
scaly-breasted-munia.migration: Largely resident; introduced feral populations established in several regions worldwide

red-avadavat.habitat: Open fields and grasslands of tropical Asia
red-avadavat.diet: Seeds and grain typical of estrildid finches
red-avadavat.migration: Largely resident within tropical Asia; breeds in the Indian subcontinent during the monsoon season

spot-billed-duck.habitat: Freshwater wetlands across the Indian subcontinent
spot-billed-duck.diet: Aquatic plants and invertebrates typical of dabbling ducks
spot-billed-duck.migration: Non-migratory resident breeding duck throughout its range

ruddy-shelduck.habitat: Inland water-bodies such as lakes, reservoirs, and rivers; nests in cliffs and tree cavities away from water
ruddy-shelduck.diet: Omnivorous; feeds on plant matter, invertebrates, and small aquatic animals
ruddy-shelduck.migration: Migratory; breeds in southeastern Europe and central Asia, winters in the Indian subcontinent; small resident populations in North Africa

bar-headed-goose.habitat: Breeds near mountain lakes in Central Asia; winters in lowland wetlands and fields of South Asia
bar-headed-goose.migration: Strongly migratory; famous for crossing the Himalayas at extreme altitudes between breeding and wintering grounds

mountain-hawk-eagle.habitat: Forest-dwelling; found in forests from the Himalayas through South and Southeast Asia to Japan and Taiwan
mountain-hawk-eagle.diet: Opportunistic predator taking birds, mammals, and reptiles
mountain-hawk-eagle.migration: Largely resident; a confirmed breeding species across its range with limited dispersal

crested-serpent-eagle.habitat: Forested habitats across tropical Asia, from the Indian subcontinent through Southeast and East Asia
crested-serpent-eagle.diet: Specialises in snakes; also takes other reptiles and small vertebrates
crested-serpent-eagle.migration: Largely resident throughout its tropical forest range

brahminy-kite.habitat: Mainly coastal and inland wetlands across the Indian subcontinent, Southeast Asia, and Australia
brahminy-kite.diet: Dead fish and other carrion; also takes live prey opportunistically

black-kite.habitat: Wide range of open and semi-open habitats across temperate and tropical Eurasia, Australasia, and Oceania
black-kite.diet: Opportunistic scavenger; carrion, refuse, insects, and small vertebrates
black-kite.migration: Temperate populations migratory, moving south in winter; tropical populations largely resident

steppe-eagle.habitat: Treeless steppe grasslands for breeding; more varied open habitats in winter including landfills and open country
steppe-eagle.diet: Specialised predator of ground squirrels when breeding; in winter feeds on insects, carrion, and easy prey
steppe-eagle.migration: Strongly migratory; entire breeding population migrates south via major flyways including the Middle East, Red Sea, and Himalayas

himalayan-monal.habitat: Himalayan forests and shrublands at elevations of 2,100–4,500 m
himalayan-monal.diet: Roots, tubers, insects, and other invertebrates dug from the ground
himalayan-monal.migration: Largely resident; descends to lower elevations in winter

kalij-pheasant.habitat: Forests and thickets, especially in the Himalayan foothills from Nepal and Pakistan to western Thailand
kalij-pheasant.diet: Seeds, roots, insects, and other forest floor invertebrates
kalij-pheasant.migration: Largely resident; may make limited altitudinal movements seasonally

golden-pheasant.size: Medium-sized pheasant; males with very long ornamental tail
golden-pheasant.habitat: Dense forests and mountainous terrain of central China

lady-amhersts-pheasant.size: Large pheasant; males with strikingly long ornamental tail
lady-amhersts-pheasant.habitat: Dense mountain forests and bamboo thickets of southwestern China and Myanmar

mikado-pheasant.size: Large pheasant with a long tail
mikado-pheasant.habitat: Dense mountain forests of Taiwan, including areas like Yushan National Park
mikado-pheasant.diet: Seeds, berries, insects, and plant matter

satyr-tragopan.habitat: Moist oak and rhododendron forests with dense undergrowth and bamboo, at 1,800–4,200 m in the Himalayas
satyr-tragopan.diet: Plant matter including leaves, shoots, berries, and insects
satyr-tragopan.migration: Altitudinal migrant; descends to around 1,800 m in winter from summer ranges up to 4,200 m

blood-pheasant.habitat: Lower Himalayas across North and East India, Nepal, Bhutan, South China, and northern Myanmar
blood-pheasant.diet: Plant matter, seeds, insects, and other invertebrates
blood-pheasant.migration: Largely resident; makes limited altitudinal movements with season

long-tailed-shrike.size: Medium-sized shrike with a notably long, narrow black tail
long-tailed-shrike.habitat: Open country, farmland, scrub, and forest edges widely across Asia
long-tailed-shrike.diet: Insects, lizards, small birds, and rodents; impales prey on thorns in typical shrike fashion
long-tailed-shrike.migration: Largely resident across most of its range; some populations make partial migrations

black-winged-stilt.size: Medium-sized wader with very long legs
black-winged-stilt.habitat: Shallow wetlands, marshes, mudflats, and lagoons across Europe, Asia, and Africa
black-winged-stilt.diet: Aquatic invertebrates, insects, and small fish picked from shallow water
black-winged-stilt.migration: Partially migratory; northern populations move south in winter, tropical populations largely resident

spotted-owlet.habitat: Open habitats including farmland, urban areas, and human habitation from Pakistan to Southeast Asia; roosts in tree hollows and rock cavities
spotted-owlet.diet: Insects, small rodents, lizards, and other small prey
spotted-owlet.migration: Largely resident throughout its tropical and subtropical range

brown-fish-owl.habitat: Forests and wooded wetlands from Turkey to South and Southeast Asia
brown-fish-owl.diet: Primarily fish; also takes crustaceans, frogs, and other aquatic prey
brown-fish-owl.migration: Largely resident throughout its wide range

vernal-hanging-parrot.habitat: Dry jungle and cultivation in the Indian subcontinent and parts of Southeast Asia
vernal-hanging-parrot.diet: Fruit, seeds, buds, blossoms, and nectar
vernal-hanging-parrot.migration: Largely resident; undergoes local movements driven by fruit and flower availability

king-vulture.habitat: Tropical lowland forests from southern Mexico to northern Argentina
king-vulture.diet: Scavenger; feeds on carrion and often makes the initial cut into fresh carcasses
king-vulture.migration: Largely resident within its tropical forest range

greater-rhea.size: Weighs 20–27 kg; the largest native bird in the Americas
greater-rhea.habitat: Open areas such as grasslands, savanna, and grassy wetlands in eastern South America
greater-rhea.diet: Broad omnivore; plants, seeds, insects, and small vertebrates

southern-cassowary.size: Large, flightless bird; one of the heaviest birds in the world
southern-cassowary.habitat: Tropical rainforests of Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, and northeastern Australia
southern-cassowary.diet: Primarily fallen fruit; also fungi, insects, frogs, and small vertebrates

hoatzin.habitat: Swamps, riparian forests, and mangroves of the Amazon and Orinoco Basins in South America
hoatzin.diet: Leaves, flowers, and fruit; digested by foregut fermentation unique among birds
hoatzin.migration: Largely resident within the Amazon and Orinoco Basins

blue-and-yellow-macaw.size: Large parrot; one of the bigger macaw species
blue-and-yellow-macaw.habitat: Forest (especially varzea), woodland, and savannah in tropical Central and South America, and Trinidad
blue-and-yellow-macaw.migration: Largely resident within its tropical range

hyacinth-macaw.size: About 1 m from head to tail tip; the longest parrot species and largest flying parrot
hyacinth-macaw.habitat: Open woodlands, palm stands, and seasonally flooded forests in central and eastern South America
hyacinth-macaw.diet: Primarily hard palm nuts cracked with its powerful beak
hyacinth-macaw.migration: Largely resident; may make seasonal movements following food availability

military-macaw.habitat: Forest and woodland from west-central Mexico south through northern Argentina and Bolivia
military-macaw.migration: Largely resident; some seasonal movements reported

sun-conure.habitat: Tropical savanna, dry forest, and palm groves in northeastern South America
sun-conure.diet: Fruits, flowers, berries, blossoms, seeds, nuts, and insects
sun-conure.migration: Largely resident within its range in northeastern South America

scarlet-ibis.habitat: Coastal mudflats, mangroves, shallow lagoons, and estuaries in tropical South America and the Caribbean
scarlet-ibis.diet: Crustaceans, molluscs, small fish, and invertebrates probed from mud
scarlet-ibis.migration: Largely resident; some populations make local seasonal movements

jabiru.size: Large stork; one of the tallest flying birds in the Americas
jabiru.habitat: Wetlands, rivers, and open areas from Mexico to Argentina; most common in the Pantanal and Eastern Chaco
jabiru.diet: Fish, frogs, reptiles, and other aquatic animals
jabiru.migration: Largely resident; makes some seasonal movements following water levels

toco-toucan.size: Largest toucan species; bill alone is conspicuously huge
toco-toucan.habitat: Semi-open habitats including cerrado, gallery forests, and wetlands up to 1,750 m in South America
toco-toucan.diet: Primarily fleshy fruits; also insects, eggs, and nestlings of other birds
toco-toucan.migration: Largely resident; range has been expanding southward in recent decades

channel-billed-toucan.habitat: Tropical forests of the Caribbean island of Trinidad and tropical South America south to Brazil and Bolivia
channel-billed-toucan.diet: Fruit, insects, and small vertebrates typical of toucans
channel-billed-toucan.migration: Largely resident within its tropical range

andean-cock-of-the-rock.habitat: Andean cloud forests in South America across a range of around 260,000 km²
andean-cock-of-the-rock.diet: Fruit supplemented by insects, amphibians, reptiles, and small mice
andean-cock-of-the-rock.migration: Largely resident within Andean cloud forests

guianan-cock-of-the-rock.habitat: Tropical rainforests, particularly near rocky outcrops in northeastern South America
guianan-cock-of-the-rock.diet: Primarily fruit; occasionally small snakes and lizards
guianan-cock-of-the-rock.migration: Largely resident in the forested region of northeastern South America

blue-crowned-motmot.habitat: Forests and woodlands; found in Mexico and Central America
blue-crowned-motmot.migration: Largely resident; nests in tunnel burrows in earthen banks

turquoise-browed-motmot.habitat: Forest edge, gallery forest, and scrubland in Central America from southeast Mexico to Costa Rica
turquoise-browed-motmot.diet: Insects and small reptiles caught from perches; occasionally fruit
turquoise-browed-motmot.migration: Largely resident throughout its Central American range

sword-billed-hummingbird.size: Among the largest hummingbirds; bill alone longer than the rest of its body
sword-billed-hummingbird.habitat: Andean regions of South America, particularly cloud forests where long-corolla flowers grow
sword-billed-hummingbird.diet: Nectar (especially from Passiflora mixta and passionflowers); also hawks insects
sword-billed-hummingbird.migration: Largely resident in the Andes; may make limited altitudinal movements

marvelous-spatuletail.size: Small hummingbird with extraordinarily elongated outer tail feathers
marvelous-spatuletail.habitat: Forest edges and shrubby habitats in northern Peru
marvelous-spatuletail.diet: Nectar and insects typical of hummingbirds
marvelous-spatuletail.migration: Largely resident; endemic to a small area of northern Peru

crimson-topaz.habitat: Tropical forests and forest edges in northern South America (Brazil, French Guiana, Guyana, Suriname, Venezuela)
crimson-topaz.diet: Nectar and insects typical of hummingbirds
crimson-topaz.migration: Largely resident within the Guiana Shield region

southern-lapwing.habitat: Open grasslands, wetland margins, and agricultural areas throughout South America, except dense Amazonian forest, high Andes, and arid western coast
southern-lapwing.diet: Insects and other invertebrates picked from the ground
southern-lapwing.migration: Largely resident and common throughout South America; expanding its range northward into Central America and the Caribbean

magellanic-penguin.habitat: Breeds on coastal Patagonia (Argentina, Chile, Falkland Islands); non-breeding birds range north to Brazil and Uruguay
magellanic-penguin.migration: Migratory; breeds in coastal Patagonia and migrates north to Brazilian and Uruguayan waters after breeding

humboldt-penguin.habitat: Rocky coasts and islands along the Pacific coast of Peru and Chile; nests in guano burrows, scrapes, and caves
humboldt-penguin.diet: Fish and squid caught by pursuit-diving
humboldt-penguin.migration: Migratory; moves along the South American Pacific coast following food availability

inca-tern.habitat: Pacific coast waters and rocky cliffs of Chile, Ecuador, and Peru
inca-tern.migration: Largely resident along the Humboldt Current coast; may make local seasonal movements

andean-flamingo.habitat: High-altitude salt lakes and lagoons in the Andes mountains of South America
andean-flamingo.diet: Algae and aquatic invertebrates filtered from shallow saline water
andean-flamingo.migration: Partially migratory; moves between Andean high-altitude lakes seasonally; often nests in shared colonies with other flamingo species

chilean-flamingo.size: 110–130 cm tall; a large flamingo slightly smaller than the greater flamingo
chilean-flamingo.habitat: Salt lagoons and soda lakes in South America from Ecuador and Peru to Chile and Argentina
chilean-flamingo.diet: Algae and aquatic invertebrates filtered from shallow water
chilean-flamingo.migration: Partially migratory; moves seasonally between breeding and feeding sites; habitat vulnerable to pollution and mining

american-flamingo.habitat: Shallow coastal lagoons, salt pans, and mudflats in the West Indies, northern South America, Galápagos Islands, and Yucatán Peninsula
american-flamingo.diet: Algae, brine shrimp, and other aquatic invertebrates filtered from shallow water
american-flamingo.migration: Largely resident within the Caribbean and northern South America; makes some seasonal movements between feeding sites

rufous-bellied-thrush.habitat: Forests, urban parks, gardens, and open woodland across eastern and southeastern Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, Uruguay, and central Argentina
rufous-bellied-thrush.migration: Largely resident; one of the most common birds in southeastern Brazil

saffron-finch.habitat: Open and semi-open areas in lowlands outside the Amazon Basin across much of South America and the Caribbean
saffron-finch.migration: Largely resident; introduced populations established in Hawaii, Panama, Puerto Rico, and Jamaica

green-headed-tanager.size: About 13.5 cm long; weighs about 18 g
green-headed-tanager.habitat: Atlantic forest in southeastern Brazil, far eastern Paraguay, and far northeastern Argentina; also common in orchards and parks
green-headed-tanager.diet: Fruit and insects foraged in the forest canopy
green-headed-tanager.migration: Largely resident within the Atlantic forest

paradise-tanager.habitat: Humid tropical and subtropical forests in the western and northern Amazon Basin (Venezuela, Peru, Colombia, Ecuador, Bolivia, Brazil)

seven-colored-tanager.habitat: Forests of northeastern Brazil; endemic to this region
seven-colored-tanager.diet: Fruit and insects typical of tanagers
seven-colored-tanager.migration: Largely resident; restricted to forests in northeastern Brazil

burrowing-parrot.habitat: Dry, open country up to 2,000 m elevation in Argentina and Chile; nests in burrows excavated in cliff faces
burrowing-parrot.migration: Partially migratory; makes seasonal movements within Argentina and Chile following food availability

monk-parakeet.habitat: Temperate to subtropical open woodland and scrub in South America; introduced populations in North America and Europe in similar climates
monk-parakeet.migration: Largely resident; only parrot to build stick nests rather than nest in cavities

great-kiskadee.size: Medium-sized passerine; one of the larger tyrant flycatchers
great-kiskadee.habitat: Open woodland with tall trees, cultivation, and areas around human habitation from southern US to Argentina
great-kiskadee.diet: Insects, small fish, frogs, and fruit; an opportunistic feeder

blue-winged-kookaburra.size: About 40 cm long; large kingfisher, slightly smaller than the laughing kookaburra
blue-winged-kookaburra.habitat: Open woodland and tropical savanna in northern Australia and southern New Guinea
blue-winged-kookaburra.diet: Insects, reptiles, small mammals, and other small prey
blue-winged-kookaburra.migration: Largely resident in northern Australia and southern New Guinea

galah.habitat: Wide variety of modified and unmodified habitats across mainland Australia; one of Australia's most widespread birds
galah.diet: Seeds, grain, roots, and green plant material foraged on the ground
galah.migration: Largely resident; highly abundant and adaptable across Australia

major-mitchells-cockatoo.habitat: Arid and semi-arid inland areas of Australia, except the northeast
major-mitchells-cockatoo.diet: Seeds, nuts, roots, and berries foraged on or near the ground
major-mitchells-cockatoo.migration: Largely resident in arid interior Australia
